summary: Updated the repo build pipeline and tools/elf2dol.c so elf2dol receives a target platform string and can account for Wii-style duplicated PDHR size handling while preserving the default GameCube build path. The Makefile now defines TARGET_COL as gamecube and passes it to ELF2DOL. elf2dol now tracks sdata and sbss sizes in two-element arrays and, when platform is "wii", also captures the sizes at sdata_pdhr + 2 and sbss_pdhr + 2 before adding all tracked sizes into the DOL BSS size.

key_files.role: Defines TARGET_COL as gamecube and passes it as an additional argument to elf2dol during DOL generation.
Adds platform-aware PDHR size tracking for sdata and sbss, with special handling for Wii by also considering PDHR indices offset by +2.

what_changed: Added TARGET_COL := gamecube near the Makefile target definitions.
Changed the DOL build rule from invoking ELF2DOL with four arguments to passing an additional platform argument: $(TARGET_COL).
Changed elf2dol's sdata and sbss size globals from single uint32_t values into two-element arrays: sdataSizes[2] and sbssSizes[2].
Extended read_elf_segments to accept a platform string and compute isWii by comparing the platform to "wii".
For sbss tracking, elf2dol now records sbssSizes[0] at sbss_pdhr and, only for Wii, sbssSizes[1] at sbss_pdhr + 2.
For sdata tracking, elf2dol now records sdataSizes[0] at sdata_pdhr and, only for Wii, sdataSizes[1] at sdata_pdhr + 2.
BSS-size adjustment now calls increment_bss_size for all four tracked slots: sdataSizes[0], sdataSizes[1], sbssSizes[0], and sbssSizes[1].
main now reads arg[4] as the platform argument and passes it through to read_elf_segments.
The 16-bit byte-swap helper was renamed from swap16 to swaf16 throughout the shown code; the diff does not explain why.
smart_moves: Kept GameCube behavior as the default Makefile path by setting TARGET_COL to gamecube, while adding a parameterized path for Wii behavior.
Avoided hardcoding Wii behavior unconditionally: the extra +2 PDHR size tracking only activates when the platform string is exactly "wii".
Represented the original and Wii-extra sdata/sbss sizes as fixed two-element arrays, which is a compact way to preserve the original single-PDHR logic while adding the second Wii slot.
Centralized the final BSS-size accounting by adding all tracked sdata/sbss sizes through the existing increment_bss_size helper rather than duplicating BSS header manipulation logic.
decomp_lessons: Build tools that encode target-specific binary layout assumptions should accept an explicit platform/target selector rather than relying on one hardcoded layout.
When extending a GameCube-oriented tool for Wii outputs, preserve the existing GameCube path and gate Wii-only offsets or segment assumptions behind an explicit platform check.
For DOL conversion, omitted or special sections such as sdata and sbss may still need to contribute to header BSS accounting even when they are not emitted as ordinary copied data.
Small Makefile variables like TARGET_COL can make future target expansion easier, but naming should be chosen carefully because the variable appears to represent platform/console rather than color.
naming_conventions: The PR introduced TARGET_COL in the Makefile with value gamecube; based on usage it functions as a target platform selector passed to elf2dol.
The C code uses isWii as a boolean-style int derived from strcmp(platform, "wii").
The prior scalar names sdataSize and sbssSize were pluralized to sdataSizes and sbssSizes after becoming arrays.

The diff renames swap16 to swaf16, including the big-endian macro and little-endian inline function. No PR text or review comments explain this spelling, so it should not be treated as an intentional naming convention without further evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR is primarily tooling/build support rather than function matching work.
The relevant binary-layout tactic is to compensate DOL header BSS size for sdata/sbss program headers that require accounting but are handled specially by elf2dol.
For Wii-style ELF layouts, the tool assumes the related sdata and sbss PDHRs may also appear two program-header indices after the configured PDHR values.
follow_up_queries: Check surrounding tools/elf2dol.c argument parsing to confirm usage text and argc validation were updated for the new platform argument.
Investigate whether the swap16 to swaf16 rename was intentional, required to avoid a symbol conflict, or an accidental typo preserved by the merge.
Search later PRs for TARGET_COL to see whether it was renamed or expanded beyond gamecube/wii.
Search later elf2dol changes for additional Wii DOL layout fixes or more general platform abstraction.
